title: roster.range (v1) — RPC request
description:
Multi-day roster lookup for the roster-adviser Unix-socket RPC. Sibling to `roster.query.v1`; saves callers from issuing N single-day queries when scheduling across a week or fortnight.

Transport: Unix socket at `/var/run/ai-roster-adviser/query.sock`. Caller authenticated via SO_PEERCRED.

Returns one row per (person, date) pair within the requested window. Each row carries the same status enum and privacy guarantees as `roster.query.v1`. ai-doer rows always return `status: 'working'`, `source: 'hardcoded'`. Cache-stale rows (per-day staleness check) come back as `status: 'unknown'` with `staleness_seconds` filled, per AP-1.

Maximum window span at v1: 30 days. Wider windows are rejected with `bad_query` to bound the receiver's response size and prevent client-side accidental fetches of the whole year.

Status: draft. Same activation gate as `roster.query.v1`.

contract_id.description: Contract identifier. Must be the literal string 'roster.range.v1'.

trace_id.description: UUIDv7 generated at the caller's entry point.

source_ref.description: URL or ID of the caller context that triggered this query.

caller_agent_id.description: Self-asserted agent ID of the caller. Authorisation decisions key off SO_PEERCRED uid.

people.description: Whose rosters to return. Order is not significant. ai-doer rows are synthesized as `status: 'working'` with `source: 'hardcoded'`. mkkk is intentionally NOT included at v1 — see roster.query.v1 description.

end.description: Inclusive ISO date. Europe/London. Maximum span at v1: 30 days from start; wider windows return `bad_query`.

window.description: Inclusive date range. The receiver returns one row per (person, date) cross product.
